symptom: MORAISim.sh always exits 127; SingleInstance relaunch exits 0 with no window; unity.lock persists after exit
hypothesis: MORAISim.sh runs launcher in backticks and executes its 'Found path:' stdout; launcher is SingleInstance mode

next: Judge normal-exit by window + Player.log, not shell exit code
resolution: Wrapper absorbs 127, guards live instance via pgrep, USE_MORAISIM=0 gives clean exit code; documented in runbook 10.2.1

symptom: Map rendered only partially/degraded on screen
hypothesis: Ruled out: NoMachine transport (local console == NoMachine minus compression) and VRAM OOM (2.6/4GB, 1.5GB free)
next: Optional mitigation: lower SIM quality/resolution; not blocking functional stages
resolution: Diagnosed as GPU-perf-bound: RTX 3050 (below min spec RTX 2060 Super) pegged 96-99%; server-side :1 capture identical to local+NoMachine, so it is the SIM's actual render output on this GPU, not a transport/config bug

symptom: CORRECTION of mis-scoped AVS-002: certain LARGE maps crash SIM on load (sangam_nobuilding 686MB scene crashes; K-City 350MB loads fine). Earlier 'partial render/gpu-perf' framing was a misread of user report
hypothesis: Vulkan Out of memory loading large scene into 4GB VRAM -> SIGSEGV in vk::DataBuffer::CreateResource / GfxDeviceVK::InitializeBufferInternal
next: Workaround: use maps that fit 4GB VRAM (K-City ok); large maps need >4GB GPU. Test lower texture/quality for headroom

symptom: Scenario Runner VTK/OpenGL GUI client area renders black in NoMachine X11 + NVIDIA offload env; only flickers on hover
hypothesis: VTK GL widget compositing failure on this X server. Ruled out: gpu-contention (black with SIM off), nomachine-capture (black on physical screen), gl-backend (black with LIBGL software swrast AND QT_XCB_GL_INTEGRATION=none)
next: Operate SR blind + verify results in SIM; revisit with newer SR/Qt or alt platform plugin

symptom: MORAI SIM hangs/crashes when its window is resized or shrunk during a session

evidence: Player.log repeated InitializeOrResetSwapChain then hang; user observed close on shrink
next: Do not resize the SIM window; keep default size
